role: Approved market data ingestion should wrap mature A-share provider candidates behind stable QuantPilot contracts.
adapter_boundary: QuantPilot owns data contracts, schema normalization, timestamp audit, provider failure handling, and safety gates; external providers supply raw market data only after approval.
why_not_directly_integrated_yet: Provider license, endpoint reliability, adjustment policy, symbol mapping, and raw-data storage gates are not approved.
next_required_action: Refresh provider gate review and choose controlled isolated provider probes; do not build a custom market data source when mature providers can be adapted.

role: Trading sessions, holidays, and exchange-day logic for A-share research and sandbox validation.
adapter_boundary: QuantPilot owns A-share rule overrides, source-versioned calendar contracts, and validation tests; external calendar libraries provide baseline sessions where license and coverage permit.
why_not_directly_integrated_yet: A-share exchange coverage, dependency weight, license posture, and exact SSE/SZSE/BSE alignment require review before adoption.
next_required_action: Evaluate mature calendar libraries before adding any custom calendar implementation.

role: Research simulation engine for signal validation before paper or capital-test readiness review.
adapter_boundary: QuantPilot owns contracts, A-share market reality checks, cost assumptions, and evidence gates; external engines remain behind adapters or isolated prototypes.
why_not_directly_integrated_yet: Prior prototypes are not enough for final selection, and no engine has proven A-share market reality, license safety, and dependency suitability.
next_required_action: R2 must define sandbox/backtest adapter contracts before any engine selection or adapter work.

role: Factor diagnostics, IC-style analysis, tear sheets, and validation support.
adapter_boundary: QuantPilot owns factor contracts, leakage gates, OOS and walk-forward policy, and A-share cost feasibility; external analytics provide diagnostics only after data gates.
why_not_directly_integrated_yet: Real data, dataframe stack, and factor validation gates are not approved; analytics packages cannot substitute for A-share realism.
next_required_action: Benchmark factor analytics candidates before expanding custom factor report code.

role: Risk, return, drawdown, turnover, and performance diagnostics for candidate review.
adapter_boundary: QuantPilot owns risk policy, capital constraints, cost assumptions, and report gating; external libraries provide metric calculations after validation.
why_not_directly_integrated_yet: Metric libraries need dependency, maintenance, and data-shape review before entering the project.
next_required_action: Compare metric coverage and dependency risk before writing custom performance analytics.

role: Position, cash, exposure, turnover, and account-state accounting for sandbox and paper tracking.
adapter_boundary: QuantPilot owns capital/account constraints, A-share lot and T+1 constraints, and audit snapshots; external systems can inform accounting models or be wrapped where safe.
why_not_directly_integrated_yet: Portfolio accounting touches capital state and execution-adjacent concepts, so direct integration requires strict review and no broker path.
next_required_action: Define portfolio/account contract boundaries and benchmark external accounting models before custom implementation.

role: Sandbox-only simulation of order feasibility, rejections, partial fills, slippage, costs, and liquidity constraints.
adapter_boundary: QuantPilot owns A-share order feasibility contracts and safety gates; external engines may provide fill-model benchmarks but cannot create broker or live execution paths.
why_not_directly_integrated_yet: A-share constraints, rejected-order reasons, and capital permissions are project-specific and must remain sandbox-only.
next_required_action: R2 should define order simulation contracts and external engine comparison points without adding execution integration.

role: Deterministic, auditable multi-agent workflow boundaries for research, validation, risk, execution gate, updater, and reporting agents.
adapter_boundary: QuantPilot owns agent contracts, safety gates, audit logs, and execution prohibitions; external runtimes may later orchestrate approved tools only.
why_not_directly_integrated_yet: Agent safety, determinism, tool permissions, and audit boundaries are not ready for runtime integration.
next_required_action: Review agent runtimes against deterministic graph control and audit requirements before implementing orchestration.

role: Project-specific A-share sandbox contract layer for T+1, T+0 eligibility where applicable, lot rules, price limits, costs, slippage, liquidity, partial fills, rejections, data latency, timestamp audit, and provider failure.
adapter_boundary: QuantPilot owns market reality contracts, A-share constraints, capital/account checks, and execution gates; external engines and libraries remain adapters, benchmarks, or prototypes.
why_not_directly_integrated_yet: No single external project covers QuantPilot's required A-share market reality, capital/account awareness, and safety-gated order-draft workflow.
next_required_action: R2 must define contracts and adapter boundaries before any sandbox implementation expands into backtest, risk, factor, or calendar core.
